intro: Cleistes humidicola, commonly known as the marsh pink orchid, is a terrestrial orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleistes humidicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleistes humidicola is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, as well as being used in the production of cough syrups and other respiratory remedies.
